search_scope.purpose: Find a source-complete non-asymmetric transition-metal benchmark candidate suitable for a formal candidate/M1 contract.
reviewed_sources: MOBH35 primary article and lawful SI lineage already reviewed for R33
bounded web discovery of open primary articles and supporting-information descriptions
databases_or_sites: Europe PMC and publisher/open-access primary pages through web discovery
limitations: Discovery snippets were not accepted as candidate facts
A finite search cannot prove that no source-complete candidate exists

promotion_criteria: Exact candidate identity and version-matched primary source
Lawfully available immutable coordinates with exact atom order
Source-reported metal oxidation and ligand-charge model or explicit reviewer closure
Exact nuclearity, coordination, ligand, substrate, counterion, and explicit-environment identities
Chemical atom mapping or equivalently reviewable intended reaction coordinate
Source-located normal-mode and path evidence status
Basis and ECP by element plus relativity provenance
Solvent, thermochemistry, standard-state, temperature, and low-frequency provenance
Candidate-specific wavefunction, stability, spin-contamination, occupation, alternative-solution, and multireference review policy

citation.title: Reaction Mechanism, Origins of Enantioselectivity, and Reactivity Trends in Asymmetric Allylic Alkylation

source_access.claim_ceiling: article-level observations only; exact SI coordinate object not ingested or hash-bound

candidate_identity.reported_step: inner-sphere C-C reductive elimination from Pd(PHOX) enolates

evidence_observations: Primary article reports ORCA 4.1.2, Pd LANL2TZ(f)/Hay-Wadt ECP, 6-31G(d) on other atoms, frequency calculations, and IRC analyses.
The article distinguishes Re/Si precursor channels and names TS20 as an enantiodetermining C-C-forming structure.
gaps: No exact lawful TS20 coordinate file and SHA-256 was ingested.
Exact candidate charge, multiplicity, oxidation-state and ligand-charge bookkeeping were not source-closed in this bounded review.
Candidate-specific imaginary mode, both IRC directions and structurally identified endpoints were not established.
Relativity, complete solvent/thermochemistry conventions and wavefunction/stability/multireference policies were not closed.

citation.title: Conformational constraints in [Ni(PR2NR'2)2] complexes for tuning H2 production and oxidation

source_access.claim_ceiling: article-level protocol observation; no exact benchmark TS selected

evidence_observations: Primary article reports B3LYP-D3, SDD ECP/basis for Ni, 6-31G(d,p) for other atoms, larger single-point basis, normal-mode analysis, and IRC calculations.
gaps: No single exact TS candidate was selected and bound to coordinates.
Charge, multiplicity, electronic-state assumptions and exact species mapping were not candidate-bound.
Candidate-specific mode and bidirectional endpoint evidence were not established.
Complete relativity, thermochemistry and wavefunction-risk policies were not closed.

citation.title: Metathesis Mechanism of Zinc-Catalyzed Fluorination of Alkenes with Hypervalent Fluoroiodine

evidence_observations: Existing OpenAlex seed retrieval identified a computational mechanism/selectivity article; no primary/SI candidate facts were accepted.
gaps: Primary article and version-matched SI were not lawfully reviewed in this bounded pass.
All exact candidate, electronic-state, coordinate, reaction-map, method and TS-evidence fields remain unverified.

selection_decision.reason: No screened alternative satisfies every mandatory candidate-bound identity, electronic-structure, coordinate, reaction-path, protocol-provenance, and wavefunction-risk criterion. Ranking reflects proximity to reviewability, not scientific acceptance.
